education.degree: Master of Science in Data Science
education.courses: Statistics, AI, Big Data Systems, Advanced NLP, Optimization, and HCI.

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering

bullets: Developing an end-to-end physics-informed ML solution to accurately predict stellar ages for both cluster and field stars, integrating astronomical constraints to ensure physically consistent model outputs.
Engineering a robust ETL pipeline using PySpark to aggregate a multi-modal dataset of 8,000+ stars, cross-matching high-dimensional catalogs for rotation periods, mass, and chemical abundance.

Designing a Mixture-of-Experts ensemble to unify gyrochronology and chemical dating methods; currently integrating Physics-Informed Neural Networks with normalizing flows to enforce astronomical consistency.

Engineered high-throughput pipelines via Apache NiFi and AWS Glue to process thousands of daily transactions, enforcing schema conformance and automated anomaly flagging to reduce downstream data errors by 30%.
Optimized 8+ .NET REST APIs and SQL stored procedures for high-concurrency, reducing dashboard P99 latency by 25% for 200+ global stakeholders.

Applied SMOTE and targeted feature engineering to address class imbalance in fraud payment datasets, improving the AUPRC and increasing fraud capture rate by 15% while maintaining false positive rate within operational thresholds.

contact.summary: I'm always open to discussing new opportunities, research collaborations, or interesting problems.

projects.title: EEG-Based Speech Reconstruction for Motor-Impaired Patients
bullets: Developed a deep learning pipeline to decode EEG signals into coherent text for individuals with severe motor impairments like ALS, achieving a 16% Word Error Rate (WER).
Engineered an end-to-end Conformer-CTC architecture to replace traditional multi-step LLM dependencies, significantly reducing model complexity and inference latency.
Optimized phonetic decoding stability by minimizing phoneme drift, resulting in a Character Error Rate (CER) that outperformed the competition's baseline.

projects.title: Exoplanet Detection using Neural Networks
bullets: Addressed a severe class imbalance in Kepler data (0.7% positive) to improve rare event detection in astronomical time-series.
Implemented SMOTE and feature modeling to handle data skew, followed by a Spiking Neural Network (SNN) and Transformer architecture to capture temporal transit patterns.
Optimized model performance via Focal Loss to penalize majority-class bias, achieving 80% precision and a 70% F1-score, representing a 6x improvement in F1 over a Logistic Regression baseline.
Reduced manual review requirements for astronomical surveys by automating the identification of planetary candidates.

projects.title: Detection of Pleural Effusion using Deep Learning
bullets: Addressed need for faster diagnosis of lung fluid in chest X-rays, reducing radiologist workload.
Applied transfer learning (InceptionV3) on 224k+ CheXpert images and fine-tuned for medical screening.
Achieved 93% accuracy (AUC 0.95) with a 5% drop in false negatives, supporting reliable large-scale quality screening.
